Keywords: value first show benefit before ask onboarding start
Purpose: Show app value through screenshots or brief interactive demo before asking for account creation
When to Use: Apps with strong visual value prop (photos design tools social); apps where sign-up friction is high
Conversion Impact: High: +25-40% trial activation; users who see value convert at 2-3× rate
iOS Convention: Show 3-5 hero screens with clear benefit statements and screenshots; no more than 4 swipe slides
Android Convention: Full-screen ViewPager with illustrated value props; indicator dots; 'Get Started' floating CTA at bottom
Anti-Pattern: Showing value-less generic illustrations (showing phone icons instead of actual app screens)

Keywords: permission priming rationale camera photos location notification
Purpose: Explain WHY permission is needed before system prompt appears to increase grant rate
When to Use: Before any system permission dialog: camera photos location notifications contacts
Conversion Impact: High: +40-60% permission grant rate vs cold system prompt; context dramatically improves trust
iOS Convention: Custom screen matching iOS style (white card centered icon title body 'Allow' button 'Not Now' secondary button); then immediately trigger system dialog
Android Convention: Custom rationale screen MD3 style before system dialog; include icon matching permission type
Anti-Pattern: Showing system permission dialog cold without any context; asking for all permissions on first launch

Keywords: progressive permission contextual request at point of need
Purpose: Request permissions only when user attempts the requiring feature; not on app launch
When to Use: All permissions that aren't instantly needed (which is most of them)
Conversion Impact: Medium-High: reduces permission anxiety; natural context = better grant rate
iOS Convention: Trigger UIAlertController or custom rationale at the moment of feature tap; back off gracefully if denied
Android Convention: Use shouldShowRequestPermissionRationale() to determine rationale need; contextual request flow
Anti-Pattern: Asking all permissions on first launch before user has context for why they're needed

Keywords: interactive tutorial coach mark tooltip feature discovery
Purpose: Guide user through core interactions by having them perform them within real UI (not sandbox)
When to Use: Feature-rich apps where key interactions are non-obvious; first 3-5 sessions
Conversion Impact: Medium: +20% D7 retention for apps with meaningful interactive tutorial vs video-only
iOS Convention: UICoachMark style tooltip with arrow; dim background; single focused step; 'Got it' dismiss; progress indicator
Android Convention: Spotlight highlight with dimmed overlay; MD3 typography for tooltip; gesture indicator animation
Anti-Pattern: Long tutorial before user can use app; skippable tutorial that hides critical info not shown elsewhere

Purpose: Allow users to explore app before committing to account creation
When to Use: Any app where core value is demonstrable without account: note-taking productivity reading
Conversion Impact: Medium: captures users who reject sign-up walls; convert to registered later after value delivery
iOS Convention: 'Skip' / 'Explore first' secondary button visible below primary CTA on all onboarding screens
Android Convention: Floating skip button top-right; low visual weight (text only no border); stores state to re-prompt later
Anti-Pattern: Hiding skip so well users can't find it; asking for account immediately after 'Skip' when entering first feature

Keywords: empty state first use zero state
Purpose: Onboarding screen embedded in the app when no data exists yet; shows what the screen will look like + how to populate
When to Use: Any screen that starts empty: tasks habits bookmarks collections
Conversion Impact: Medium: +15-25% activation; empty state with CTA drives first data creation
iOS Convention: Illustration (SF Symbols or custom) + headline + body + large CTA button; .frame(maxWidth: 280) centered
Android Convention: Empty state with Material illustration + 'Get Started' extended FAB or CTA button; centered layout
Anti-Pattern: Generic 'No items found' text with no illustration or CTA; showing error icon instead of encouraging illustration

Keywords: social proof rating reviews users count testimonial
Purpose: Show credibility signals (rating downloads user count testimonials) early in onboarding to build trust
When to Use: Free apps competing in crowded categories; apps where trust is key (health finance)
Conversion Impact: Medium: +10-20% signup completion; particularly effective for cold audience (paid traffic)
iOS Convention: App Store rating badge (4.8★); user count '100K+ users'; centered layout; before signup CTA
Android Convention: Similar but use Google Play rating; 5-star visual indicator; horizontal review cards scrollable
Anti-Pattern: Fake or unverifiable social proof; reviews without attribution; showing inflated stats that damage trust later

Purpose: Defer mandatory account creation until after user has experienced app value or created content
When to Use: Apps where user generates data before needing sync: journaling notes fitness tracking
Conversion Impact: High: +30-50% completion vs upfront signup; user has more motivation after creating content
iOS Convention: Guest mode → 'Save your progress' prompt after first content creation → native signup (Sign in with Apple primary)
Android Convention: Google Sign-In as primary; email secondary; progress loss warning before first close
Anti-Pattern: Requiring account on first launch before any value; showing signup after 30 seconds of app open with no engagement

Keywords: sign in apple social auth login quick
Purpose: Streamline authentication with one-tap Sign In with Apple on iOS reducing friction
When to Use: All iOS apps with account creation — required by Apple if any third-party social login offered
Conversion Impact: High: +40% signup completion vs email form; users trust Apple auth and share less data
iOS Convention: Required if other social logins exist; Apple button must be at same prominence as other social buttons; follow HIG button styling
Android Convention: Optional on Android since not native; use Google Sign-In as Android equivalent
Anti-Pattern: Violating Apple's requirement to offer Sign in with Apple alongside other social options (App Store rejection risk)

Keywords: paywall timing delay freemium trial premium
Purpose: Show paywall after user has experienced value over multiple sessions; not on first launch
When to Use: Subscription apps with trials; after user completes key 'aha moment' actions
Conversion Impact: High: Best performing paywall timing is after 3rd+ session or after key value delivery
iOS Convention: UIViewController presented as sheet; trial offer prominently shown; 'Start Free Trial' primary; 'Maybe later' secondary visible
Android Convention: BottomSheetDialog or full-screen Activity; trial duration in prominent font; feature list with checkmarks; Google Play billing
Anti-Pattern: Showing paywall on first open or after only 30 seconds; hiding 'No thanks' / 'Maybe later' CTA

Keywords: coach mark spotlight tip bubble overlay
Purpose: Highlight a single new or non-obvious feature with a focused tooltip overlay
When to Use: New feature launches; power features that provide value but require discovery
Conversion Impact: Medium: +15% feature adoption for featured capability
iOS Convention: UICoachMark style: semi-transparent dark overlay with highlighted cutout; tooltip bubble; tap anywhere to dismiss
Android Convention: Spotlight with MotionLayout animation; MD3-styled tooltip; single step preferred; part of a sequence
Anti-Pattern: More than 5-7 coach marks in sequence; coach marks on every launch; auto-advancing coach marks too fast

Keywords: biometric face id touch id fingerprint login setup
Purpose: Prompt to enable biometric authentication (Face ID/Fingerprint) for secure quick login
When to Use: After account creation or first successful password login; apps with sensitive data
Conversion Impact: Medium-High: biometric enables users to stay logged in increasing retention
iOS Convention: LocalAuthentication framework; NSFaceIDUsageDescription; system prompt; custom pre-prompt explaining convenience
Android Convention: BiometricPrompt API; prompt after PIN setup; 'Use fingerprint to log in faster'
Anti-Pattern: Forcing biometric without password fallback; prompting for biometric before user has created account

Keywords: notification prompt contextual timing push permission
Purpose: Request notification permission at moment user would clearly benefit from notifications
When to Use: After key user action that creates notification-eligible content: task created appointment set order placed
Conversion Impact: High: contextual request = +50-80% grant vs launch request; user understands benefit
iOS Convention: Custom rationale card + system prompt; 'Stay updated on [specific thing user just did]' copy
Android Convention: Custom rationale + system dialog (Android 13+); show specific example notification
Anti-Pattern: Requesting notifications on first screen with generic 'Enable notifications for the best experience' copy

Keywords: skeleton loading placeholder first load appears
Purpose: Show skeleton/shimmer placeholder UI that matches expected content shape during initial data load
When to Use: Any screen with network-fetched content; better UX perception than blank screen or spinner for >300ms load
Conversion Impact: Medium: improves perceived performance; users who see skeleton complete first-time flows at higher rate
iOS Convention: Gray gradient shimmer matching content row shapes; 200ms delay before showing (avoid flash for fast loads)
Android Convention: Shimmer animation matching MaterialCardView shapes; fade-in when real content loads
Anti-Pattern: Generic spinner in center of screen; showing 'Loading...' text without skeleton; skeleton shape not matching real content

Keywords: onboarding quiz personalization questions customize experience
Purpose: Ask 2-4 questions to personalize initial experience and increase engagement; users feel product is made for them
When to Use: Health fitness learning finance apps where personalization meaningfully changes content
Conversion Impact: Medium-High: +20-35% D30 retention for apps with meaningful personalization vs generic start
iOS Convention: Simple card-style questions; progress bar; visual answer options (icons + text); max 4 questions; skip option on each
Android Convention: MD3 RadioButton or Card selection; full-width answer cards; visible step indicator
Anti-Pattern: 10+ question quiz; forcing answers without skip; using quiz purely for marketing data without actual personalization

Keywords: google sign in social auth android login
Purpose: One-tap Google Sign-In for quick Android authentication reducing form friction
When to Use: Android apps with account creation — Google Sign-In is equivalent of Sign in with Apple for Android
Conversion Impact: High: +35% Android signup completion vs email form; pre-filled with Gmail info
iOS Convention: Google Sign-In available on iOS (GIDSignIn SDK) but lower priority behind Apple; show below Sign in with Apple
Android Convention: Google Identity Services (GIS) / Credential Manager API; one-tap sign in; Google button follows brand guidelines
Anti-Pattern: Using deprecated GoogleApiClient; custom Google button that doesn't match brand guidelines
